title_en: 32. Anas (radhiAllaahu'anhu) narrates that the Prophet (sallallaahu'alaihi wa sallam),
title_ar: الحديث الثاني والثلاثون: رواية أنس رضي الله عنه
content_en: when he made wudoo , used to take a handful of water and enter it below his chin and rub it through his beard and said This is what my Lord - the Great and Exalted - has ordered me to do. [Abo Dawood (no.145), al-Baihaqee (1/54), alHaakim (1/149). Ash-Shaikh al-Albaani declares it to be sahih (sahih ul-Jaami', no.4572)]. Some of the scholars have declared that entering water through the beard is obligatory and say: If he deliberately leaves it - then he must repeat the ( wudoo and) prayer. And this is the saying of Imam Ishaaq and Abu Thaur. Most of the scholars however are of the opinion that the order is an order of desirability (istihbaab) and it is not obligatory - and that it is clearer that that which is obligatory is moving water through that part of the beard..so that it reaches the skin underneath. [Al-Khattaabee, 1/56]. Imam Ahmad and Laith and most of the scholars hold that moving water through the beard is obligatory when taking ghusl from Janaabah - and not obligatory in wudoo . ['Aun al Ma'bood, 1/247]. WASHING THE ARMS INCLUDING THE ELBOWS Note: The arms here which we are ordered to wash begin with the fingertips and hands - which are to be included in this washing - they being part of the arm (yad) which we are ordered to Wash in the aayah. Allaah ta'ala says: O Ye who believe! When ye prepare for prayer, wash your faces, and your hands (and arms) to the elbows; rub your heads (with water); and (wash) your feet to the ankles. [soorat-ul-Maa'idah,5:7] info@calgaryislam.com www.calgaryislam.com Humraan ibn Abaan reports that 'Uthmaan (radhiAllaahu'anhu) called for water for wudoo and mentioned the Prophet's (sallallaahu 'alaihi wa sallam) way of making wudoo - and Humraan said: Then he washed his right arm including the elbow three times, then the left in the same way. [Al-Bukhaaree and Muslim as has preceded]. Regarding inclusion of the elbows in the washing of the arms - scholars have differed: Some of them saying that they must be included in this washing, and others saying that this is not so. And this difference of opinion is due to their disagreement over the meaning of the word "ilaa" (to) in the aayah - does it mean 'up to/until' or 'up to and including' ? He who says that it means 'up to' does not include the elbows in the washing - just as Allaah ta'ala says: ... thumma 'atimmus-siyaama ilaa -llail: Then complete your fast till the night appears; [soorat-ul-Baqara, 2:187]. And this is the opinion of some of the companions of Imam Maalik. Most scholars, however, hold the view that the meaning is 'up to and including/along with' - and thus include the elbows in the washing and use as their evidence Allaah ta'ala's saying: ...wa yayazidkum quwwatan ilaa quwwatikum...: ...and add strength to your strength; [Soorat-ul-Hud 11:52]
title_id: Hadis 32: Hukum Meratakan Air pada Jenggot dan Membasuh Lengan
content_id: Anas (radhiyallahu anhu) meriwayatkan bahwa Nabi ﷺ ketika berwudu, biasa mengambil satu telapak air dan memasukkannya di bawah dagunya lalu meratakan melalui jenggotnya, dan bersabda: Beginilah yang Tuhanku yang Maha Agung dan Maha Mulia memerintahkanku. [Abu Dawud (no. 145), Al-Baihaqi (1/54), Al-Hakim (1/149). Syaikh Al-Albani menyatakannya sahih (Sahih al-Jami, no. 4572)]. Sebagian ulama menyatakan bahwa memasukkan air melalui jenggot adalah wajib dan berkata: Jika seseorang meninggalkannya dengan sengaja, ia harus mengulangi wudu dan salatnya. Ini adalah pendapat Imam Ishak dan Abu Tsaur. Namun mayoritas ulama berpendapat bahwa perintah itu merupakan anjuran (istihbab) dan bukan kewajiban. Yang lebih jelas adalah bahwa kewajiban itu adalah mengalirkan air pada bagian jenggot agar air sampai ke kulit di bawahnya. [Al-Khattabi, 1/56]. Imam Ahmad, Lais, dan mayoritas ulama berpendapat bahwa memasukkan air melalui jenggot adalah wajib ketika mandi janabah, bukan dalam wudu. [Aun al-Mabud, 1/247]. MEMBASUH LENGAN TERMASUK SIKU. Catatan: Lengan yang diperintahkan untuk dibasuh dimulai dari ujung jari dan tangan, yang ikut termasuk dalam pembasuhan ini, karena keduanya adalah bagian dari yad (tangan/lengan) yang diperintahkan untuk dibasuh dalam ayat. Allah Taala berfirman: Wahai orang-orang yang beriman, apabila kamu hendak mendirikan salat, maka basuhlah wajahmu dan tanganmu sampai ke siku. [Surah Al-Maidah 5:6]. Humran ibn Aban meriwayatkan bahwa Utsman (radhiyallahu anhu) meminta air untuk wudu dan menyebutkan cara Nabi ﷺ berwudu. Humran berkata: Kemudian beliau membasuh lengan kanannya termasuk siku tiga kali, lalu lengan kiri dengan cara yang sama. [Al-Bukhari dan Muslim sebagaimana telah disebutkan]. Mengenai apakah siku ikut dibasuh, para ulama berbeda pendapat: sebagian mengatakan siku harus ikut dibasuh, sebagian lain mengatakan tidak. Perbedaan ini disebabkan perbedaan penafsiran kata "ila" (sampai) dalam ayat, apakah bermakna "sampai pada" atau "sampai pada dan termasuk". Mayoritas ulama berpendapat maknanya adalah "sampai pada dan termasuk", sehingga siku ikut dibasuh.
content_ar: عندما he made الوضوء , used to take a handful of water and enter it below his chin and rub it through his beard and said This is what my Lord - the Great and Exalted - has ordered me to do. [Abo Dawood (no.145), al-Baihaqee (1/54), alHaakim (1/149). Ash-Shaikh al-Albaani declares it to be صحيح (صحيح ul-Jaami', no.4572)]. Some of the العلماء have declared that entering water through the beard is obligatory and say: إذا he deliberately leaves it - then he must repeat the ( الوضوء and) الصلاة. And this is the saying of الإمام Ishaaq and Abu Thaur. Most of the العلماء غير أن are of the opinion that the order is an order of desirability (istihbaab) and it is not obligatory - and that it is clearer that that which is obligatory is moving water through that part of the beard..لذا that it reaches the skin underneath. [Al-Khattaabee, 1/56]. الإمام أحمد and Laith and most of the العلماء hold that moving water through the beard is obligatory عندما taking الغسل from Janaabah - and not obligatory in الوضوء . ['Aun al Ma'bood, 1/247]. WASHING THE ARMS بما في ذلك THE ELBOWS Note: The arms here which we are ordered to wash begin with the fingertips and hands - which are to be included in this washing - they being part of the arm (yad) which we are ordered to Wash in the aayah. Allaah ta'ala says: O Ye who believe! عندما ye prepare for الصلاة, wash your faces, and your hands (and arms) to the elbows; rub your heads (with water); and (wash) your feet to the ankles. [soorat-ul-Maa'idah,5:7] info@calgaryislam.com www.calgaryislam.com Humraan ibn Abaan reports that 'Uthmaan (radhiAllaahu'anhu) called for water for الوضوء and mentioned النبي ﷺ's (ﷺ) الطريق of making الوضوء - and Humraan said: Then he washed his الحق arm بما في ذلك the elbow three times, then the left in the same الطريق. [البخاري and مسلم as has preceded]. فيما يخص inclusion of the elbows in the washing of the arms - العلماء have differed: Some of them saying that they must be included in this washing, and others saying that this is not لذا. And this difference of opinion is بسبب their disagreement over the meaning of the word "ilaa" (to) in the aayah - does it mean 'up to/حتى' or 'up to and بما في ذلك' ? He who says that it means 'up to' does not include the elbows in the washing - just as Allaah ta'ala says: ... thumma 'atimmus-siyaama ilaa -llail: Then complete your fast till the night appears; [soorat-ul-Baqara, 2:187]. And this is the opinion of some of the الصحابة of الإمام Maalik. Most العلماء, غير أن, hold the view that the meaning is 'up to and بما في ذلك/along with' - and وهكذا include the elbows in the washing and use as their evidence Allaah ta'ala's saying: ...wa yayazidkum quwwatan ilaa quwwatikum...: ...and add القوة to your القوة; [Soorat-ul-Hud 11:52]
